meta.title: Rajabhakti Park — The Complete Visitor Guide
meta.description: Rajabhakti Park, Hua Hin — your complete guide: history, royal statues, visitor tips and real reviews. Experience the grandeur of Thailand's royal history.

hero.subtitle: A historically themed park honouring past Thai kings from the Sukhothai period to the current royal house of Chakri, built by the Royal Thai Army.

about.label: — 02 · About the Park —
about.heading: About Rajabhakti Park
about.lead: A monumental historical park in Hua Hin, created to honor the great kings of Thailand.
about.paragraph1: Rajabhakti Park is a historically themed park honouring past Thai kings. It is located in Hua Hin, Prachuap Khiri Khan Province, Thailand. The park was built by the Royal Thai Army with funds donated by the public and private sectors.
about.paragraph2: King Bhumibol Adulyadej gave the historical park the name 'Rajabhakti Park', which means 'the park that has been built with people's loyalty to the monarchs'.
about.quote: Rajabhakti Park stands as a testament to the enduring reverence the Thai people hold for their monarchs.

highlights.title: Royal Statues
highlights.desc: Seven magnificent bronze statues of Thailand's greatest kings

highlights.title: Plaza
highlights.desc: A vast ceremonial plaza used for important national events

highlights.title: Museum
highlights.desc: Educational exhibits about the history of the Thai monarchy

editorTip.text: Visit in the late afternoon (16:00–18:00) to avoid the midday heat and enjoy beautiful sunset views.

history.label: — 03 · History & Legacy —
history.heading: The History & Legacy of the Kings
history.intro: The park honors seven notable Thai kings who made significant contributions to the nation.

blocks.title: Sukhothai Period
blocks.text: King Ram Khamhaeng the Great (reigned 1279-1298) is honored for his creation of the Thai alphabet and establishing Buddhism as the state religion.

blocks.title: Ayutthaya Period
blocks.text: King Naresuan the Great (1590-1605) and King Narai the Great (1656-1688) are celebrated for defending the kingdom and expanding its diplomatic and commercial relations.

blocks.title: Thonburi and Rattanakosin Periods
blocks.text: King Taksin the Great (1767-1782) reunified the kingdom. From the Chakri dynasty, King Rama I (1782-1809), King Mongkut (1851-1868), and King Chulalongkorn (1868-1910) are honored for their roles in modernizing Thailand and preserving its independence.

legend.title: The Meaning of Rajabhakti
legend.text: The name 'Rajabhakti' was graciously bestowed by King Bhumibol Adulyadej (Rama IX), signifying the profound loyalty and devotion of the people to the monarchy.

didYouKnow.title: Did you know?
didYouKnow.text: Casting of the statues and construction of the multi-purpose plaza took just 10 months, from November 2014 to August 2015.

kings.name: King Ram Khamhaeng the Great
kings.desc: Created the Thai alphabet and established Buddhism as the state religion.

kings.name: King Naresuan the Great
kings.desc: Declared independence for Ayutthaya and expanded the kingdom's territory.

kings.name: King Narai the Great
kings.desc: Fostered immense diplomatic and commercial relations with European nations.

kings.name: King Taksin the Great
kings.desc: Reunified the kingdom after the fall of Ayutthaya.

kings.name: King Rama I (Phutthayotfa Chulalok)
kings.desc: Founded the Chakri Dynasty and established Bangkok as the capital.

kings.name: King Rama IV (Mongkut)
kings.desc: Initiated the modernization of Siam and embraced Western science.

kings.name: King Rama V (Chulalongkorn)
kings.desc: Abolished slavery, modernized the government, and preserved Thailand's independence.

guide.label: — 04 · Visitor Guide —
guide.heading: Rajabhakti Park Visitor Guide
guide.lead: Essential information to help you plan your visit to this historical monument.

items.title: Park entry
items.desc: Free — no gate fee for all visitors

items.title: Tram service
items.desc: Available for a small fee for those who prefer not to walk

items.title: Private car
items.desc: Located on Phet Kasem Road (Highway 4), about 8 km south of Hua Hin town center.

items.title: Public transport
items.desc: Green songthaews (shared taxis) run from Hua Hin town to Khao Takiab, from where you can take a taxi or walk.

items.title: Train
items.desc: Suan Son Pradiphat Railway Halt is situated 790 metres east of Rajabhakti Park.

items.title: Tour packages
items.desc: Many Hua Hin day tours include a stop at Rajabhakti Park.

faqs.a: No, entry to the park is completely free for both Thai nationals and foreigners.

faqs.q: Is there shade from the sun?
faqs.a: The main plaza is very open and exposed to the sun. It is highly recommended to bring an umbrella, hat, and sunscreen, especially if visiting midday.
Yes, the park is very spacious and accessible with ramps.
Flying drones is strictly prohibited without prior official permission from the military authorities.

etiquette.text: As Rajabhakti Park is managed by the Royal Thai Army and is a place of deep respect honoring the monarchy, visitors must dress modestly. Sleeveless shirts, short skirts, and shorts above the knee are strictly prohibited. Please maintain a respectful demeanor, especially when taking photographs.

itineraries.title: Half-Day History Tour
itineraries.desc: Start at Hua Hin Railway Station (classic Thai architecture), proceed to Rajabhakti Park for historical insights, and end at Khao Takiab for a panoramic coastal view.

itineraries.title: Full-Day Cultural Route
itineraries.desc: Morning at Maruekhathaiyawan Palace, afternoon at Rajabhakti Park, and sunset at Suan Son Pradiphat Beach.

food.label: — 05 · Facilities & Amenities —
food.heading: Park Facilities
food.lead: Comfortable amenities to ensure a pleasant visit for all guests.

dishes.name: Visitor Center
dishes.desc: Information desk and historical exhibition area

dishes.name: Souvenir Shop
dishes.desc: Official park merchandise and local handicrafts

dishes.name: Refreshment Stalls
dishes.desc: Cold drinks, ice cream, and light snacks available near the parking area

quote.text: The park provides a serene and respectful environment for visitors to reflect on the nation's history.
quote.author: — Park Administration

gallery.label: — 06 · Photo Gallery —
gallery.heading: Scenes of Rajabhakti Park
gallery.intro: A visual journey through the monumental statues and the grand plaza of the park.
gallery.historicalNote: A modern tribute to ancient glory: The park perfectly blends contemporary bronze casting techniques with traditional Thai artistic styles.
gallery.credits: Photos are shared under Creative Commons licenses for non-profit educational use.
alts: The seven bronze statues of Thai kings
Close-up of King Ram Khamhaeng statue
The park against the backdrop of Hua Hin mountains
Night illumination of the royal statues
Panoramic view of the park grounds

reviews.label: — 07 · Visitor Reviews —
reviews.heading: Voices of Our Visitors
reviews.intro: A collection of opinions from travellers around the world who visited Rajabhakti Park.
reviews.declaration: All reviews are genuine user opinions with no sponsorship

featured.text: An awe-inspiring sight. The sheer scale of the statues and the mountain backdrop make for a majestic experience.
A must-visit in Hua Hin to understand Thai history and the deep respect for the monarchy.

items.name: Somchai T.
items.text: Very proud as a Thai to visit this place. The statues are magnificent.

items.name: Anna K.
items.text: Impressive monument, but very hot during the day. Come around 5 PM.

items.name: Wichai P.
items.text: Beautifully maintained grounds and very clean facilities.

items.name: Liu Mei
items.text: Great place for photos, the mountain background is stunning.

items.name: John D.
items.text: Interesting historical site. Read up on the kings before you go to appreciate it more.

items.name: Pimchanok S.
items.text: Very peaceful atmosphere. The tram service is helpful for the elderly.

items.name: Tanaka H.
items.text: Grand scale. Remember to wear appropriate clothing (no shorts).

items.name: Niran R.
items.text: A solemn and impressive place. Easy to access from Hua Hin town.

reviews.disclaimer: Overall rating 4.4 from 11,056 reviewers on Google Maps

mapSection.label: — 08 · Location & Getting There —
mapSection.heading: Location & Getting There
mapSection.lead: Rajabhakti Park is located in Nong Kae, Hua Hin District, Prachuap Khiri Khan Province.
mapSection.address: FXX8+H8H, 8 Phet Kasem Rd, Nong Kae, Hua Hin District, Prachuap Khiri Khan 77110, Thailand
